claims.flip_evidence_text: except, in the case of clauses (i), (ii), (vi), (vii) and (viii) to the extent such Effect has a materially disproportionate adverse effect on the Company and its Subsidiaries, taken as a whole, relative to others in the industries and geographical regions in which affected businesses of the Company and its Subsidiaries operate in respect of the business conducted in such industries and applicable geographical regions.
claims.notes: Omits clause (vi) (changes in Law or GAAP); the relativity language applies to five clauses, not four.

prompt:
You are a proxy legal annotator for a research pilot. Use only the contract excerpt, the question, the model answer, and the fixed claim list.

For each claim return:
- correctness_label: true, false, or partially_true
- load_bearing_label: yes or no
- flip_evidence_text: required if load_bearing_label=yes, else empty string
- notes: optional short note

Contract excerpt:
“Material Adverse Effect” means any Effect that, individually or in the aggregate with all other Effects, (a) has had or would be reasonably expected to have a material adverse effect on the business, condition (financial or otherwise), properties, assets, liabilities (contingent or otherwise), business operations or results of operations of the Company and its Subsidiaries, taken as a whole or (b) would or would reasonably be expected to prevent, materially delay or materially impair the ability of the Company to consummate the Merger or to perform any of its obligations under this Agreement by the Outside Date; provided, however, in the case of clause (a) no Effect arising out of or resulting from any of the following shall be deemed either alone or in combination to constitute a Material Adverse Effect: (i) changes or conditions generally affecting the industries in 10 which the Company and any of its Subsidiaries operate, (ii) general economic or political conditions (including U.S.-China relations), commodity pricing or securities, credit, financial or other capital markets conditions, in each case in the United States or any foreign jurisdiction in which the Company or any of its Subsidiaries operate, (iii) any failure, in and of itself, by the Company to meet any internal or published projections, forecasts, estimates or predictions in respect of revenues, earnings or other financial or operating metrics for any period (it being understood that the facts or occurrences giving rise to or contributing to such failure may be deemed to constitute, or be taken into account in determining whether there has been, or is reasonably expected to be, a Material Adverse Effect, to the extent permitted by this definition), (iv) consequences resulting from the execution and delivery of this Agreement and/or the Original Merger Agreement or the public announcement or pendency of the transactions contemplated hereby or thereby, including the impact thereof on the relationships, contractual or otherwise, of the Company or any of its Subsidiaries with employees, labor unions, customers, suppliers, designers, landlords or partners, (v) any change, in and of itself, in the market price or trading volume of the Company’s securities or in its credit ratings (it being understood that the facts or occurrences giving rise to or contributing to such change may be deemed to constitute, or be taken into account in determining whether there has been, or is reasonably expected to be, a Material Adverse Effect, to the extent permitted by this definition), (vi) any change in Law applicable to the Company’s business or GAAP (or authoritative interpretation thereof), (vii) geopolitical conditions, the outbreak or escalation of hostilities (including the Hong Kong protests and the “Yellow Vest” movement), any acts of war (whether or not declared), sabotage (including cyberattacks) or terrorism, or any escalation or worsening of any such acts of hostilities, war, sabotage or terrorism threatened or underway from the date of the Original Merger Agreement through to the date of this Agreement, (viii) any hurricane, tornado, flood, earthquake or other natural disaster, (ix) any actions required to be taken or not taken by the Company or any of its Subsidiaries (other than the Company’s obligations under the first sentence of Section 7.1(a)) pursuant to this Agreement or the Original Merger Agreement, or, with Parent’s prior written consent (whether granted hereunder or pursuant to the Original Merger Agreement), (x) any Effect described in any of the Company Reports filed prior to the date of this Agreement, any written communication delivered by the Company to Parent pursuant to the Original Merger Agreement or discussed in Parent’s filings with the Court of Chancery of the State of Delaware in connection with the Merger Litigation or (xi) any outbreak of a virus, infectious disease, other contagion or public health event (including COVID-19 and any COVID-19 Measures), except, in the case of clauses (i), (ii), (vi), (vii) and (viii) to the extent such Effect has a materially disproportionate adverse effect on the Company and its Subsidiaries, taken as a whole, relative to others in the industries and geographical regions in which affected businesses of the Company and its Subsidiaries operate in respect of the business conducted in such industries and applicable geographical regions. (Pages 16-17)

Question:
Relational language (MAE) applies to

Llama answer:
The relativity language in the Material Adverse Effect (MAE) definition applies to clauses (i), (ii), (vii), and (viii), which relate to general industry conditions, general economic or political conditions, geopolitical conditions, and natural disasters, respectively, to the extent such effects have a materially disproportionate adverse effect on the Company and its Subsidiaries relative to others in the industries and geographical regions in which affected businesses operate.

Canonical claims:
- claim-6a231a1dddcb: The relativity language in the Material Adverse Effect (MAE) definition applies to clauses (i), (ii), (vii), and (viii), which relate to general industry conditions, general economic or political conditions, geopolitical conditions, and natural disasters, respectively, to the extent such effects have a materially disproportionate adverse effect on the Company and its Subsidiaries relative to others in the industries and geographical regions in which affected businesses operate.

message.reasoning:
The user wants me to evaluate a claim against a contract excerpt, question, and model answer.

 Contract excerpt defines "Material Adverse Effect" and includes a carve-out with an exception at the end: "except, in the case of clauses (i), (ii), (vi), (vii) and (viii) to the extent such Effect has a materially disproportionate adverse effect on the Company and its Subsidiaries..."

 The Llama answer says the relativity language applies to clauses (i), (ii), (vii), and (viii).

 The canonical claim is identical to the Llama answer.

 Wait, the contract says clauses (i), (ii), (vi), (vii) and (viii). The Llama answer and claim omit clause (vi) which is "any change in Law applicable to the Company’s business or GAAP (or authoritative interpretation thereof)".

 So the claim is partially true because it correctly identifies (i), (ii), (vii), (viii) but misses (vi).

 Is the claim load bearing? The question is "Relational language (MAE) applies to". The answer should list the clauses. Missing (vi) is a material omission, so the claim is not fully correct. Is it load bearing? The "flip_evidence_text" would be the text showing that (vi) is also included. The claim is about which clauses the relativity language applies to. The contract explicitly lists (i), (ii), (vi), (vii), (viii). The claim says (i), (ii), (vii), (viii) — omitting (vi). This is a factual error. Since the omission makes the claim incomplete/wrong, and the evidence that flips it is the contract text showing (vi) is included, this is load bearing.

 Wait, I need to determine if it's "partially_true" or "false". It gets 4 out of 5 clauses correct. That's partially_true. It's load bearing because the missing clause (vi) is material to the answer.

 Let me double-check the contract text: "except, in the case of clauses (i), (ii), (vi), (vii) and (viii) to the extent such Effect has a materially disproportionate adverse effect..."

 Yes, (vi) is definitely there.

 So:
 - correctness_label: partially_true
 - load_bearing_label: yes
 - flip_evidence_text: "except, in the case of clauses (i), (ii), (vi), (vii) and (viii) to the extent such Effect has a materially disproportionate adverse effect on the Company and its Subsidiaries, taken as a whole, relative to others in the industries and geographical regions in which affected businesses of the Company and its Subsidiaries operate in respect of the business conducted in such industries and applicable geographical regions."
 - notes: Omits clause (vi) (changes in Law or GAAP).

 Wait, the user says "For each claim return...". There is only one claim listed: claim-6a231a1dddcb.

 I need to format the output. Usually these tasks want JSON or a structured format. Let me provide it clearly.

 Let me reconsider "load_bearing_label". A claim is load-bearing if the evidence that contradicts or supports it is directly in the text and changes the correctness. Here, the text explicitly lists (vi), which the claim omits. This is a direct textual contradiction (by omission), so yes, it's load bearing. The flip evidence is the sentence showing (vi) is included.

 Actually, is omission considered "flip evidence"? Yes, because the text shows the correct list includes (vi), which flips the claim from seeming true to being partially true/false.

 Let me check if there's any ambiguity. The Llama answer says "clauses (i), (ii), (vii), and (viii)". The contract says "(i), (ii), (vi), (vii) and (viii)". The claim is identical to the Llama answer. The question is "Relational language (MAE) applies to". The model answer should presumably be the correct list from the contract.

 So:
 correctness_label: partially_true
 load_bearing_label: yes
 flip_evidence_text: the excerpt showing (vi) is included.
 notes: The relativity language also applies to clause (vi) (changes in Law or GAAP), which is omitted.

 I should make sure the flip_evidence_text is exact from the contract. I'll quote the relevant phrase.

 "except, in the case of clauses (i), (ii), (vi), (vii) and (viii) to the extent such Effect has a materially disproportionate adverse effect on the Company and its Subsidiaries, taken as a whole, relative to others in the industries and geographical regions in which affected businesses of the Company and its Subsidiaries operate in respect of the business conducted in such industries and applicable geographical regions."

 That seems right.

 One final check: Is it possible the question implies something else? "Relational language (MAE) applies to" — this is asking to which exceptions the disproportionality exception applies. The contract clearly says (i), (ii), (vi), (vii), (viii). The claim misses (vi). So partially_true is correct.

 I'll format as JSON.
